Object.Name: Syslog
Object.Description1: This object represents the syslog functionality of a device, enabling storage, retrieval, and optional remote forwarding of log messages following syslog conventions as defined in RFC 5424.

Item.Name: Log Entry
Item.Description: A syslog message entry stored on the device. Each instance represents one log message, typically in RFC 5424 or RFC 3164 format. The device maintains a circular buffer of recent entries.

Item.Name: Log Level
Item.Description: Minimum severity level for log messages to be recorded, as defined by RFC 5424. Messages with a severity numerically less than or equal to this value are logged. 0: Emergency - System is unusable. 1: Alert - Action must be taken immediately. 2: Critical - Critical conditions. 3: Error - Error conditions. 4: Warning - Warning conditions. 5: Notice - Normal but significant condition. 6: Informational - Informational messages. 7: Debug - Debug-level messages.

Item.Name: Syslog Server URI
Item.Description: URI of the remote syslog collector to which log messages are forwarded (e.g., udp://192.168.1.1:514 or tls://logs.example.com:6514). Used only when Remote Logging Enabled is true.

Item.Name: Remote Logging Enabled
Item.Description: When set to true, the device forwards log messages to the remote syslog server specified in Syslog Server URI.

Item.Name: Clear Logs
Item.Description: Executing this resource clears all stored log entries from the device log buffer.

Item.Name: Max Log Entries
Item.Description: The maximum number of log entries retained in the circular buffer. When the buffer is full, the oldest entry is overwritten by the newest.

Object.Description2: Log Level values follow RFC 5424 severity levels: 0=Emergency, 1=Alert, 2=Critical, 3=Error, 4=Warning, 5=Notice, 6=Informational, 7=Debug. A lower numeric value indicates higher severity.
